title: P1-2: standing vendor-drift test — templates byte-identical to .bee/bin
action: Fix review P1-2. Add a standing test to templates/tests/test_lib.mjs (near the existing vendored-source NUL/C0 sweep) that byte-compares every helper the onboarding copy step vendors: each skills/bee-hive/templates/*.mjs against .bee/bin/<same-name>.mjs, and each templates/lib/*.mjs against .bee/bin/lib/<same-name>.mjs. Enumerate by readdir of the templates dirs (new helpers are covered automatically — mirror how onboard_bee.mjs listTemplateHelpers enumerates), skip names with no .bee/bin sibling ONLY when .bee/bin itself is absent (a bare checkout); in this repo a missing sibling is a FAILURE (the drift the test exists to catch). Failure message names the drifted file and says to re-copy the template over the vendored copy.

truths: A one-byte divergence between any template helper and its .bee/bin sibling turns the suite red naming the file
A template helper with no vendored sibling fails in this repo
Enumeration is readdir-driven — a future new template is covered with no test edit

artifacts.substantive: readdir-driven byte-equality sweep over templates vs .bee/bin

key_links: Covers both templates/*.mjs and templates/lib/*.mjs including guards.mjs, state.mjs, bee_state.mjs, bee_backlog.mjs
prohibitions: Do not weaken any existing assertion
Do not touch .bee/ vendored files in this cell

trace.outcome: Added a readdir-driven standing test to test_lib.mjs that byte-compares every skills/bee-hive/templates/*.mjs and templates/lib/*.mjs against its .bee/bin sibling, mirroring onboard_bee.mjs's listTemplateHelpers/listTemplateLibModules enumeration and copy_helper/copy_lib path mapping; missing sibling or byte mismatch fails naming the file, empty sweep result fails, absent .bee/bin (bare checkout) is the only skip.
trace.verification_evidence:
{
  "tests_inspected": ["skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs (existing vendored-source NUL/C0 sweep near end of file, and onboard_bee.mjs listTemplateHelpers/listTemplateLibModules/copy_helper/copy_lib for the enumeration+mapping contract)"],
  "tests_added_or_changed": ["skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs: new check 'vendored source: every templates/*.mjs and templates/lib/*.mjs is byte-identical to its .bee/bin sibling' (1 case, readdir-driven over both dirs)"],
  "red_failure_evidence": "Reverted the working tree to the pre-change test_lib.mjs (git show HEAD:...), appended a byte to .bee/bin/bee_state.mjs to simulate one-sided drift, and ran the old suite: 148 passed, 0 failed — the drift went completely undetected, reproducing the exact P1-2 gap ('a future one-sided edit goes green forever'). Restored bee_state.mjs, then reapplied the new test_lib.mjs and reran with the same drift injected: 148 passed, 1 failed, naming 'bee_state.mjs: templates/bee_state.mjs and .bee/bin/bee_state.mjs have diverged (byte mismatch) — re-copy the template over the vendored copy.'",
  "verification_run": "node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs -> 149 passed, 0 failed (clean repo, all templates/lib byte-identical to .bee/bin)",
  "deliberate_exceptions": []
}
trace.verify_output: 149 passed, 0 failed (includes new check: vendored source: every templates/*.mjs and templates/lib/*.mjs is byte-identical to its .bee/bin sibling). Negative-control proof: appended a byte to .bee/bin/bee_state.mjs -> suite went to 148 passed, 1 failed naming bee_state.mjs: templates/bee_state.mjs and .bee/bin/bee_state.mjs have diverged (byte mismatch) -- re-copy the template over the vendored copy.; file restored, rerun back to 149 passed, 0 failed.
